frameworks.name: probabilistic model of change detection
frameworks.one_line: Formal probabilistic account of change detection with structured memory

frameworks.name: perceptual grouping / Gestalt organization
frameworks.one_line: Items grouped perceptually rather than treated independently in VWM

frameworks.name: slot/independent-item VWM models
frameworks.one_line: Standard capacity models that ignore higher order structure

frameworks.name: scene gist encoding
frameworks.one_line: Summary-level scene information complements detailed object encoding

core_question: Do visual working memory models need higher order structure and perceptual grouping to explain change detection?
key_assumptions: Observers encode both item-level detail and summary-level information
Perceptual organization can bind items into groups within VWM
Change detection performance reflects the underlying memory representation
manipulations: Standard random VWM displays vs displays with patterned item arrangements
Manipulating presence of higher order regularities across items
key_findings: Independent-item models fail to fully account for change detection performance
Models incorporating perceptual grouping fit human change detection better
Models adding higher order summary encoding are necessary for patterned displays
interpretations: VWM representations are richer and more structured than slot/independent-item accounts assume
Capacity estimates must account for higher order structure to generalize to natural scenes
